calendarIntegrations.title: Calendar Integrations
calendarIntegrations.notConfiguredDesc: Add your Google OAuth Client ID to the environment to enable Google Calendar import.
calendarIntegrations.note: Imported events appear as busy blocks in your schedule. The scheduler will not place tasks on top of them.

auth.loginSubtitle: Log in to your AXIS account
auth.termsNotice: By signing up you agree to our terms of service.
auth.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.

landing.heroSubtitle: AXIS intelligently schedules your tasks around your energy, deadlines, and working hours — so you never have to wrestle with your calendar again.
landing.ctaTitle: Ready to stop fighting your schedule?
landing.ctaSubtitle: Sign up free and let the engine build your week.

features.title: Cognitive-aware scheduling
features.desc: Deep work, moderate, and light tasks are placed based on your energy windows — not just availability.

features.title: Three scheduling modes
features.desc: Flexible, anchor, and fixed modes give you precise control over how and when tasks are placed.

features.title: Recurring task support
features.desc: Daily, weekday, weekly, or custom intervals. The engine handles expansion and conflict detection automatically.

features.title: Lock and protect blocks
features.desc: Lock any block to keep it in place across rebuilds. The engine works around what you've committed to.

features.title: Day, week & month views
features.desc: Drag, resize, and navigate your schedule with a familiar calendar interface built for power users.

features.title: Deadline-aware engine
features.desc: Tasks are scored by priority and deadline urgency, ensuring the right work lands at the right time.

vision.title: More than a scheduler.
vision.titleHighlight: A system for your entire life.
vision.subtitle: AXIS is being built in four interconnected engines. The Time Engine is the first step. Each phase deepens the system's understanding of who you are and what you need.

engines.name: Time Engine
engines.desc: Intelligent scheduling and automatic prioritization of your tasks.

engines.name: Body Engine
engines.desc: Track nutrition, sleep, movement, and recovery to understand your capacity.

engines.name: Mind Engine
engines.desc: Journal, track mood and habits, and surface the patterns behind your behavior.

engines.name: Life OS
engines.desc: The intelligence layer that connects all engines and guides your growth.

featuresPage.pageTitle: Everything you need to own your schedule
featuresPage.pageSubtitle: AXIS is built for people who take their work seriously — and need their tools to keep up.

items.title: Cognitive-aware scheduling engine
items.desc: The scheduling engine understands your energy. Deep work tasks land in your high-focus window. Moderate tasks fill the afternoon. Light admin goes wherever it fits. Your calendar starts to match how you actually think and work.

items.title: Three scheduling modes
items.desc: Flexible mode lets the engine decide placement based on deadlines and energy. Anchor mode pins a task to a preferred time window. Fixed mode locks a task to an exact date and time. Use the right mode for each task — not the same mode for everything.

items.title: Smart chunking and execution styles
items.desc: Long tasks can be split across multiple sessions automatically with auto_chunk mode. Set a single large block with single mode, or manually define a multi-session split. The engine respects min and max chunk sizes from your settings.

items.title: Recurring tasks
items.desc: Define daily, weekday, weekly, or custom-interval recurring tasks. The engine expands them across your scheduling window and handles conflicts automatically without duplicating blocks.

items.title: Lock and rebuild
items.desc: Lock any block to freeze it in place. When you hit Rebuild Schedule, the engine replans everything else around your locked commitments. Nothing is disrupted that you told it not to touch.

items.title: Day, week, and month views
items.desc: Switch between a detailed day view, a full week grid with 15-minute snap, and a month overview. Drag blocks to move them. Resize from the bottom edge. Double-click any slot to quick-add a task.

items.title: Deadline and priority scoring
items.desc: The engine scores every task by priority level (1–5) and deadline urgency, placing high-stakes tasks earlier and giving breathing room to tasks with distant deadlines.

items.title: Configurable working hours and limits
items.desc: Set your working hours, your deep work window, buffer time between tasks, and daily limits for deep and total hours. The engine never schedules outside your boundaries.

items.title: Cloud sync with Supabase
items.desc: Your tasks, blocks, and settings are stored in your own Supabase account with row-level security. Everything is scoped to your user — no one else can read your data.

faqPage.pageSubtitle: Everything you need to know about AXIS.

items.q: How does the scheduling engine work?
items.a: AXIS's engine takes all your active tasks, respects your locked blocks, and builds a schedule that fits within your working hours and energy windows. Tasks are scored by priority and deadline urgency, then placed into the best available slots based on their energy requirement.

items.q: What is the difference between Flexible, Anchor, and Fixed modes?
items.a: Flexible lets the engine choose when to place the task. Anchor pins the task to a preferred time window (e.g., every morning 9–11am) but allows some flexibility. Fixed locks the task to an exact date and time — the engine will not move it.

items.q: What happens when I click "Rebuild Schedule"?
items.a: The engine recalculates your entire schedule. It keeps all blocks you have locked in place and replans everything else from scratch based on your current tasks and settings.

items.q: Can I use AXIS without an account?
items.a: The app requires an account to store your data in the cloud. Without an account, data is only kept in browser localStorage and will be lost if you clear your browser data or switch devices.
Yes. Your data is stored in a Supabase database with row-level security enabled. Only your account can read or write your tasks, blocks, and settings — no other user can access them.

items.q: Can I lock a block so it does not get moved during rebuilds?
items.a: Yes. Click the lock icon on any block to freeze it. When you rebuild, the engine works around all locked blocks.

items.q: How does recurring task support work?
items.a: When you create a recurring task, the engine expands it across your scheduling window based on the recurrence pattern (daily, weekdays, weekly, or a custom interval). Each instance is treated as a separate schedulable unit.

items.q: What are "energy levels" and how do they affect scheduling?
items.a: Deep tasks require high focus and are placed in your designated deep work window (e.g., 8am–12pm). Moderate tasks go in mid-energy periods. Light tasks (admin, reviews) are placed in low-energy slots. You can configure your deep work window in Settings.

items.q: Can I drag and resize tasks on the calendar?
items.a: Yes. In the week and day views, you can drag any block to move it and drag from the bottom edge to resize it. Moved or resized blocks are automatically locked in place.

items.q: Can I use AXIS on mobile?
items.a: The app is optimized for desktop use. A mobile-friendly version may be added in the future.

roadmap.heroSubtitle: AXIS begins with time — and grows into a complete system for understanding and improving every dimension of how you live.
roadmap.heroDesc: Each engine is designed to work alone. Together, they form something fundamentally more powerful: a system that actually knows you.

engines.name: Time Engine
engines.description: The Time Engine is the foundation of AXIS. It handles the cognitive overhead of scheduling so you can focus entirely on doing the work.
features: Intelligent auto-scheduling based on priority and deadline
Energy-aware time blocks matched to your natural rhythms
Flexible, anchor, and fixed scheduling modes
engines.why: Most people fail not from lack of ambition, but from the gap between deciding and doing. The Time Engine closes that gap by converting your intentions into a concrete, conflict-free schedule — and rebuilding it whenever life changes.
engines.bridge: Your schedule is only as effective as the energy behind it. Body Engine measures that.

engines.name: Body Engine
engines.mission: Understand the physical state behind your performance.
engines.description: The Body Engine brings your health data into the system — not as a fitness app, but as a lens through which AXIS understands your capacity and energy on any given day.
engines.why: Your physical state is not a separate concern — it is the foundation of everything else. When your body is depleted, even the best schedule becomes aspirational fiction. When it is strong, the same schedule becomes achievable. AXIS needs to know the difference.
engines.bridge: Physical recovery shapes emotional state. Mind Engine captures what happens next.

engines.name: Mind Engine
engines.mission: Know yourself deeply enough to improve.
engines.description: The Mind Engine is your space for inner clarity. It helps you track how you actually feel — not just what you do — and surfaces the emotional and behavioral patterns that shape everything else.
engines.why: Plans fail for emotional reasons more often than logistical ones. The Mind Engine gives you the language to understand why some weeks feel like progress and others like drift. It turns subjective experience into legible data — the kind AXIS can actually learn from.
engines.bridge: Time, Body, and Mind together form a complete picture. Life OS reads it — and helps you write a better future.

lifeOS.name: Life OS
lifeOS.mission: An intelligent guide that connects every dimension of your life.
lifeOS.description: Life OS is not a fourth tracker. It is the intelligence layer that sits above the other three engines — reading the full picture they create together, and using it to help you understand yourself and improve.
lifeOS.description2: On its own, each engine gives you data. Life OS gives you meaning. It finds the connections between how you spend your time, how your body feels, and how your mind is doing — and turns that into something actionable: a clearer understanding of where you are, and a realistic path to where you want to be.

capabilities.title: Pattern recognition across engines
capabilities.desc: Life OS reads across your schedule, your physical state, and your inner life to find patterns that no single engine could see alone.

capabilities.title: Trajectory analysis
capabilities.desc: Based on your current habits and direction, Life OS shows you where your behavior is likely to lead — and whether that destination matches what you actually want.

capabilities.title: Early course correction
capabilities.desc: It notices when you're drifting before the drift becomes a problem — and names it clearly so you can decide what to do about it.

capabilities.title: Intelligent suggestions
capabilities.desc: Life OS can propose concrete changes across the engines: a better sleep window, a lighter workload during recovery periods, a journaling habit that fits your existing routine.

capabilities.title: Conversational guidance
capabilities.desc: Ask it questions. "Why do I keep skipping workouts?" "Am I making progress toward my goals?" "What would a better week look like?" It answers with your own data, not generic advice.

pillars.title: An intelligent mirror
pillars.desc: Life OS reflects your patterns back to you clearly and honestly — not to judge, but to help you see what's actually happening.

pillars.title: A thoughtful guide
pillars.desc: It offers suggestions, not mandates. You stay in control of every decision. The system proposes; you choose.

pillars.title: Built around you
pillars.desc: Every insight comes from your own data — not generic averages or population benchmarks. The more you use it, the more precisely it understands your specific patterns.

cta.title: Start building your foundation.
cta.subtitle: The Time Engine is available now. Everything begins here — with your schedule, your priorities, and your time.
